source.title: Reward-motivated learning: mesolimbic activation precedes memory formation.

confidence_rationale: Direct evidence that NAcc anticipation has a downstream functional consequence -- predicting subsequent memory formation 24h later. This is the load-bearing finding for REE because it ties the candidate VALENCE_EXCITEMENT write to schema/memory etching, exactly the function EXQ-538 sleep-on ablation is designed to test. Cross-tags with SD-017 because the memory-formation -> sleep-consolidation pipeline is the same loop the V3 sleep substrate would consolidate over.
failure_signatures: Anticipatory NAcc activation in trials with HIGH-reward cues did NOT improve memory if the activation was below threshold; the effect is graded with anticipatory-affect amplitude. A binary excitement-on/off channel would miss the graded effect.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Anticipatory mesolimbic activation (VTA, NAcc, hippocampus) preceding scene encoding predicts which scenes will be remembered 24h later. Trial-by-trial NAcc-anticipation amplitude on HIGH-reward cues correlates with subsequent memory; functional connectivity between hippocampus and VTA at encoding correlates with declarative memory formation.
mapping.ree_translation: VALENCE_EXCITEMENT, if written at cue presentation during a reward-anticipation episode, would predict where in z_world memory consolidation should be prioritised. The Adcock et al. finding directly motivates wiring VALENCE_EXCITEMENT into MECH-205 surprise-gated replay write path: locations recorded with high excitement get higher replay priority during sleep cycles. This is a concrete functional payoff distinct from anything existing channels do.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Healthy adult humans, declarative memory of scenes; REE memory is a different substrate (residue field + ContextMemory + RBF nodes). The principle (anticipatory positive arousal predicts subsequent memory) translates, but the specific write site needs to be designed. Also, the effect is graded -- a binary excitement signal would lose information.
mapping.source_context: Healthy adult humans, fMRI during MID-task encoding paradigm with delayed memory recognition test.

confidence_components.notes: Strong source (Neuron, well-cited). High mapping fidelity for the function (anticipatory-affect drives memory consolidation); the architectural translation to REE residue + sleep replay is direct.
